title: Phase 1 spec: tools â€” agent/task spawning (AgentTool, Task*, SendMessage, AskUserQuestion) [CC parity]
description:
## Goal

Extract a behavioral spec for this subsystem by reading Claude Code's TypeScript source. **Output**: update this issue body with a markdown spec pinning down each testable contract (input â†’ expected output, edge cases, error paths). No Rust yet â€” that is Phase 2.

Parent audit: #523.

## Claude Code source â€” read these

- `tools/AgentTool/runAgent.ts` (~973 lines)
- `tools/TaskCreateTool/TaskCreateTool.ts` (~138 lines)
- `tools/TaskGetTool/TaskGetTool.ts` (~128 lines)
- `tools/TaskStopTool/TaskStopTool.ts` (~131 lines)
- `tools/SendMessageTool/SendMessageTool.ts` (~917 lines)
- `tools/AskUserQuestionTool/prompt.ts` (~44 lines)

## OpenClaudia counterpart

- `src/subagent.rs` (~1567 lines) â€” `run_in_background` at 1311-1322, `resume` at 925
- `src/tools/task.rs` (~152 lines)
- `src/tools/ask_user.rs` (~233 lines)
- **missing** â€” no TaskOutputTool (poll background agent result) â€” filed as gap #574
- **missing** â€” no SendMessageTool â€” filed as gap #577
- **missing** â€” no TeamCreateTool/TeamDeleteTool â€” filed as gap #578

## Behavioral spec

### 1. `task` (Agent) with `run_in_background: true` returns an `agent_id` immediately

**CC**: `tools/AgentTool/runAgent.ts:248-329` â€” `runAgent()` is an async generator; the AgentTool call site passes `isAsync: true` when background. The caller yields nothing to the parent until completion because it spawns on the task loop. The AgentTool.tsx call returns a result with the new `agentId` (a UUID created by `createAgentId()` at line 347) to the parent tool result immediately; the generator runs independently with `agentAbortController` unlinked from the parent (lines 524-528).

**OC**: `src/subagent.rs:1310-1335` â€” `execute_task_tool()`: when `run_in_background == true`, calls `BACKGROUND_AGENTS.register(agent_type, description)` then `Handle::spawn(async { run_subagent(...) })`. Returns immediately with: `"Background agent started with ID: {agent_id}\nTask: {description}\nType: {agent_type:?}\n\nUse agent_output with this agent_id to retrieve results."` and `is_error: false`.

**Input**: `{ description: String, prompt: String, subagent_type: String, run_in_background: true }`.

**Output**: A plain-text string containing the `agent_id` (UUID), task description, and type. `is_error = false`.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: If `Handle::try_current()` fails, falls back to new runtime. OC does the same (lines 1338-1345).
- CC: Parent abort signal is NOT linked; async agent has its own `AbortController` (runAgent.ts:524-528). OC: same â€” spawned task runs independently.
- CC: Returns `AgentId` (typed UUID); OC returns opaque string. Format differs â€” CC uses `createAgentId()` (typed wrapper), OC uses `BACKGROUND_AGENTS.register()` which generates its own ID.

**Error path**: CC: if `AgentId` generation fails, throws during setup. OC: if `Handle::try_current()` returns `Err` and runtime creation fails, returns `("Failed to create runtime: {e}", true)`.

---

### 2. `task` with `resume: <agent_id>` continues from previous subagent context

**CC**: `tools/AgentTool/runAgent.ts:248-329` (override.agentId path) + `tools/AgentTool/resumeAgent.ts` (companion). CC reads the sidechain transcript from disk (`utils/sessionStorage.ts:recordSidechainTranscript`), re-injects as `forkContextMessages`. Agent metadata (`worktreePath`, `description`, agentType) is restored from `writeAgentMetadata` at lines 738-742.

**OC**: `src/subagent.rs:664-701` â€” `run_subagent()` resume branch: if `config.resume_agent_id` is `Some`, calls `load_transcript(resume_id)`. On success, loads `prev_messages` and appends a new user message: `"Continuing from where you left off.\n\n{prompt}"`. A **new** agent_id is registered via `BACKGROUND_AGENTS.register()` (the old one is NOT reused â€” line 668). On failure, returns `SubagentResult { success: false, output: "No transcript found for agent '{id}'. It may have expired (TTL: {N} minutes)." }`.

**Input**: `{ description: String, prompt: String, subagent_type: String, resume: "<agent_id>" }`.

**Output** (success): runs the continued conversation; result is the same as a fresh `task` invocation (text summary of completion). **Output** (miss): `is_error = true`, message contains "No transcript found" and TTL hint.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: resumes with exact same `agentId` to preserve transcript continuity and prompt-cache. OC: **generates a new agent_id on resume** â€” this is a behavioral divergence from CC. Filed as discovered gap #582.
- OC: transcript has a TTL (`TRANSCRIPT_TTL_SECS`); CC transcripts persist in `~/.claude/projects/` with no hard TTL.
- CC: worktree path is restored from metadata. OC: no metadata restoration â€” resumed agent starts fresh system prompt without worktree context.

**Error path**: Missing transcript â†’ `is_error = true`; agent type mismatch on resume not validated in OC.

---

### 3. `task_create` with `blocked_by` enforces non-transition to `in_progress` while blockers are pending

**CC**: `utils/tasks.ts:584-593` â€” `claimTask()`: checks `task.blockedBy` against unresolved task IDs (those with status â‰  `'completed'`). Both `pending` and `in_progress` blockers count as unresolved. Returns `{ success: false, reason: 'blocked', task, blockedByTasks }`. The `TaskCreateTool.ts:80-108` itself does not set `blocked_by` at create time (only `blocks: [], blockedBy: []`); the dependency is set later via `TaskUpdateTool`. Enforcement happens at claim time, not create time.

**OC**: `src/session/task.rs:186-260` â€” `update_task()`: when `status = InProgress` is requested, demotes any other currently-in-progress task to `Pending` (lines 218-223) but does **not** check `blocked_by` list before allowing the transition. `create_task()` (lines 112-135) initializes `blocked_by: Vec::new()` â€” no blocker is set at create time.

**Input**: `task_create({ subject, description })` then `task_update({ task_id, add_blocked_by: ["task-1"] })` then `task_update({ task_id, status: "in_progress" })`.

**Output CC**: transition to `in_progress` blocked if any task in `blockedBy` is not `completed`. Returns `{ success: false, reason: 'blocked', blockedByTasks: [...] }`.

**Output OC**: transition to `in_progress` **succeeds** even with pending blockers â€” the blocked_by enforcement is missing. This is a behavioral gap.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: a blocker that is itself `in_progress` still blocks the dependent â€” only `completed` resolves. OC has no enforcement at all.
- CC: cycle detection exists at `addTaskBlocking()` (utils/tasks.ts). OC: cycle detection exists at `src/session/task.rs:150-168` (`would_create_cycle`).

**Error path CC**: `reason: 'blocked'` with list of blocking task IDs. **Error path OC**: none â€” silently allows the transition.

---

### 4. `ask_user_question` suspends tool loop, delivers question to TUI, resumes on response

**CC**: `tools/AskUserQuestionTool/AskUserQuestionTool.tsx:155-241` â€” `requiresUserInteraction()` returns `true`; `checkPermissions()` returns `{ behavior: 'ask', message: 'Answer questions?' }`. The `call()` function receives pre-filled `answers` dict (injected by the UI layer after the user responds) and returns `{ data: { questions, answers, annotations? } }`. The tool does not block the loop itself â€” the permission/interaction machinery suspends the query loop until the user submits.

**OC**: `src/tools/ask_user.rs:14-132` â€” `execute_ask_user_question()` emits a JSON blob with marker `"type": "user_question"` and the normalized questions array. `src/cli/repl/plan_mode.rs:287-293` and `src/cli/repl/input.rs:6-121` handle the marker: the CLI loop detects the marker in the tool result, calls `handle_user_questions()` which blocks on `stdin.read_line()` for each question, returns a JSON `answers` map, which is then fed back to the conversation as the tool result.

**Input**: `{ questions: Array<{ question: String, header: String (â‰¤12 chars), options: Array<{ label, description, preview? }> (2-4), multiSelect?: bool }> }` â€” 1-4 questions.

**Output CC**: `{ questions, answers: Record<questionText, string|string[]>, annotations?: Record<questionText, { preview?, notes? }> }`.

**Output OC**: JSON `answers` map `Record<questionText, string|string[]>` fed back as tool result string.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: `preview` field on options triggers side-by-side UI layout. OC: `preview` is validated (must be string, `src/tools/ask_user.rs:98-106`) but **not rendered** â€” TUI is non-visual for preview content. Filed as discovered gap #591.
- CC: `annotations` (per-question notes from user) passed back. OC: no annotations support.
- Both: always append an implicit "Other" option allowing free-text input.
- OC normalizes `multi_select` â†’ `multiSelect` canonical spelling (`src/tools/ask_user.rs:115-124`).
- CC: HTML preview validation (`validateHtmlPreview`) rejects `<html>/<body>/<script>` tags. OC: no HTML validation â€” only type check.

**Error path**: CC: `validateInput` rejects duplicate question texts and duplicate option labels (AskUserQuestionTool.tsx refine). OC: same checks enforced at `src/tools/ask_user.rs:28-107` â€” duplicate question text (`seen_question_texts`) and duplicate option labels (`seen_labels`), header > 12 chars, options not 2-4. Missing `question`/`header`/`options`/`label`/`description` â†’ `is_error = true` with descriptive message.

---

### 5. `task_stop` on background agent terminates the tokio task and marks status `stopped`

**CC**: `tools/TaskStopTool/TaskStopTool.ts:60-130` â€” `validateInput()`: requires `task_id` or legacy `shell_id`; checks `appState.tasks[id]` exists and `task.status === 'running'` (errorCode 3 if not running, errorCode 1 if not found or missing ID). `call()` delegates to `stopTask(id, { getAppState, setAppState })` which aborts the task's AbortController and updates status. Returns `{ message, task_id, task_type, command }`.

**OC**: `src/subagent.rs` â€” **`task_stop` tool does not exist**. `BACKGROUND_AGENTS` registry has no `.stop()` or `.cancel()` method. Background tokio tasks run to completion or failure with no external abort path. Filed as confirmed gap (behavior #5 â€” see gap #580).

**Input CC**: `{ task_id: String }` (or deprecated `shell_id`).

**Output CC**: `{ message: "Successfully stopped task: {id} ({command})", task_id, task_type, command }`.

**Edge cases CC**:
- `shell_id` accepted as alias for `task_id` (backward compat with deprecated KillShell tool).
- Task must be `status: 'running'` â€” not-running tasks return errorCode 3.
- Missing both `task_id` and `shell_id` â†’ errorCode 1.

**Error path CC**: not found â†’ `"No task found with ID: {id}"`, errorCode 1. Not running â†’ `"Task {id} is not running (status: {status})"`, errorCode 3.

**OC**: missing â€” filed as gap #580.

---

### 6. `task_get` on unknown `agent_id` returns explicit not-found error

**CC**: `tools/TaskGetTool/TaskGetTool.ts:73-97` â€” `call({ taskId })`: calls `getTask(taskListId, taskId)`; if task not found, returns `{ data: { task: null } }`. The `mapToolResultToToolResultBlockParam` renders this as `"Task not found"` string (lines 100-105).

**OC**: `src/tools/task.rs:104-112` â€” `execute_task_get()`: calls `task_mgr.get_task(task_id)`; if not found (`None`), returns `(format!("Task '{task_id}' not found"), true)` â€” i.e., `is_error = true`.

**Input**: `{ task_id: String }`.

**Output CC**: `{ task: null }` â†’ rendered as `"Task not found"` (non-error, `is_error = false`).

**Output OC**: `("Task '{task_id}' not found", true)` â€” `is_error = true`.

**Edge cases**:
- CC: returns structured `{ task: null }` â€” the model sees a valid result with null task. OC: returns an error string â€” the model sees a tool error. These are semantically different; OC treating not-found as an error could cause retry loops.
- Missing `task_id` argument: CC â€” Zod schema rejects at validation. OC: `execute_task_get()` returns `("Missing 'task_id' argument", true)` (line 105).

**Error path**: Both return a human-readable message for not-found. CC: non-error (`is_error = false`, task = null). OC: error (`is_error = true`). This is a behavioral difference â€” CC is "graceful null", OC is "hard error".

---

### 7. Multi-select vs single-select `ask_user_question` responses are parsed correctly

**CC**: `tools/AskUserQuestionTool/AskUserQuestionTool.tsx:209-241` â€” `answers` dict is pre-filled by the UI before `call()` executes. Single-select: `answers[questionText] = "OptionLabel"` (string). Multi-select: `answers[questionText] = ["Label1", "Label2"]` (array of strings). The `mapToolResultToToolResultBlockParam` formats as `"questionText"="answer"` â€” arrays are joined.

**OC**: `src/cli/repl/input.rs:62-118` â€” single-select: parses user's numeric input, looks up option label, stores as `Value::String(label)`. Multi-select (`multi_select` bool): splits on commas, maps each number to option label, stores as `Value::Array(selected)`. "Other" option always available as `options.len() + 1`.

**Input** (single): user types `"2"` â†’ answer is the label of option 2.
**Input** (multi): user types `"1,3"` â†’ answer is `["LabelA", "LabelC"]`.
**Input** (other): user types the "Other" number â†’ prompted for free text â†’ stored verbatim.

**Output CC**: `answers: { "Question text": "Selected label" }` or `{ "Question text": ["Label1", "Label2"] }`.

**Output OC**: same shape â€” `answers` map with string or array values returned as JSON string fed back to conversation.

**Edge cases**:
- OC: if input cannot be parsed as a number (e.g., user types text directly), it is stored verbatim as a string (line 112-114 of `input.rs`). CC: the UI layer handles non-numeric input â€” always structured.
- CC: multi-select disabled for questions with `preview` content (`prompt.ts:19`). OC: no such restriction â€” `preview` + `multiSelect: true` is accepted by the validator (`src/tools/ask_user.rs:186-200`).
- OC: the `input.rs` multi-select handler reads the **legacy** `multi_select` key (line 19 of `input.rs`) rather than the normalized `multiSelect` key. This creates a bug: the validator normalizes to `multiSelect` (canonical), but `handle_user_questions()` reads `multi_select` â†’ multi-select never activates. Filed as discovered gap #585.

**Error path**: Invalid selections out of range: OC silently skips (no value added for that selection). CC: UI prevents invalid selection.

---

## Gaps already visible from the brief

These are CC features OpenClaudia does not implement. Each has been filed as a separate issue.

- **TaskOutputTool missing** â€” filed as gap #574 â€” "Add `task_output` tool to poll incremental output of background agents"
- **SendMessageTool missing** â€” filed as gap #577 â€” "Add `send_message` tool for cross-agent message passing (parity with CC `tools/SendMessageTool`)"
- **Team tools missing** â€” filed as gap #578 â€” "Add `team_create`/`team_delete` tools"

## Additional discovered gaps

The following behaviors present in CC were discovered during the spec read and are absent from OC. Each has been filed as a separate issue.

- **`task_stop` tool missing** â€” filed as gap #580 â€” OC has no mechanism to abort a running background tokio task. CC's `TaskStopTool` aborts via `AbortController` and updates status to `stopped`.
- **Resume re-uses old agent_id in CC; OC generates new one** â€” filed as gap #582 â€” on `resume`, CC re-uses the original `agentId` for transcript continuity and prompt-cache hits. OC always allocates a new ID.
- **`ask_user_question` multi-select key bug** â€” filed as gap #585 â€” `handle_user_questions()` in `src/cli/repl/input.rs:19` reads legacy `multi_select` key but the validator normalizes all output to canonical `multiSelect`. Multi-select is silently never activated after validation.
- **`task_get` not-found returns error instead of null** â€” filed as gap #588 â€” CC returns `{ task: null }` (non-error); OC returns `is_error = true`. Semantically different â€” agents using CC behavior will retry on OC.
- **Preview annotations not supported** â€” filed as gap #591 â€” CC `ask_user_question` returns `annotations` (per-question user notes on preview selections); OC has no annotations pass-through.
- **blocked_by not enforced on InProgress transition** â€” filed as gap #593 â€” OC `TaskManager::update_task()` allows transition to `InProgress` even when `blocked_by` tasks are pending/in_progress. CC enforces this at claim time.

## Done when

- This issue body has been edited to contain a markdown spec, one section per behavior above
- Each behavior section names the function(s) on both sides (or marks the OC side missing)
- Every gap above has been filed as its own issue and linked here
- The matching Phase 2 issue is unblocked
